UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO MEET THE BURDEN UNDER THE FIRST PRONG AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 4, 7, 8, AND 9, WHICH ARE "BASED EXCLUSIVELY ON ALLEGATIONS OF HABITABILITY DEFECTS AND DECREASED SERVICES" (REPLY 5:20-23). CAUSES OF ACTION 2 AND 3 ARE AT LEAST "MIXED CAUSES OF ACTION" IN PART BASED ON NON-PROTECTED ACTIVITY. (COMPLAINT PARAGRAPHS 78-86; 94). PLAINTIFFS MEET THEIR BURDEN TO SHOW PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING ON THE MERITS AS TO THE IMPLIED BREACH OF WARRANTY OF HABITABILITY AND DEFENDANT'S VIOLATION OF CC 1940.6. WALLACE V. MCCUBIN (2011) 196 CAL.APP.4TH 1169. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER APPROVED AS TO FORM. JUDGE LILIAN SING, CLERK: BERTHA PAREDES; REPORTER JUDITH A. DEALBA CSR. 5709 (501)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-17-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Richard Stephen Diestel, Esq of Bledsoe, Diesel LLP (415-981-5411) for 1049 Market Starret LLC m Amy Bogart, Terry Bogart, John Gall, Richard Lane, Robert Mullen, Halley Bogart, SFOL management Co. Shi HO, Inc. Defendants and moving parties.
Jacqueline Ravenscroft Esq. and Joseph s. Tobener, J.D. M.S. of Tobener Law Center (415-504-2165) for Victor Arreola, et al Plaintiffs and opposing party.

Argued. The court adopts the tentative rulings as follows: DEFENDANTs 1049 MARKET STRRET LLC, AMY BOGART, TERRY BOGART, JOHN GALL, RICHARD LANE, ROBERT MULLEN, HALLEY BOGART, SFOL MANAGEMENT COMPANY and SHI HO, INC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is DENIED. Defendant failed to meet the burden under the first prong as to causes of action 4, 7, 8, and 9, which are "based exclusively on allegations of habitability defects and decreased services" (Reply 5:20-23). Causes of action 2 and 3 are at least "mixed causes of action" in part based on non-protected activity. (Complaint Paragraphs 78-86; 94). Plaintiffs meet their burden to show probability of prevailing on the merits as to the implied breach of warranty of habitability and Defendant's violation of CC 1940.6. Wallace v. McCubin (2011) 196 Cal.App.4th 1169. Prevailing party to prepare order approved as to form. Judge Lilian Sing, Clerk: Bertha Paredes; reporter Judith A. DeAlba CSR. 5709 (501)
